programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Weekend food show full of mouth-watering food, great chefs and celebrity guests Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Georgina Hayden and Richard Bainbridge, and by special guests Nish Kumar and the Great British Menu winner. Helen McGinn picks the wines to go with the studio dishes.

programme.title: Flaming Feasts
programme.desc: Chris 'Flamebaster' Roberts dives into Cardigan’s wild west. Coracle fishing, driftwood crab feasts and wood-fired calzones bursting with local flavour. Chris explores the real Wrexham, from historic lager and five-generation fruit farms to vegan pioneers, Portuguese flavours and a fire-cooked feast celebrating local produce.

programme.title: Football Focus: 25.04.2026 11:00.
programme.desc: A preview of all the top footballing action with detailed analysis from an expert panel of guests. It’s FA Cup semi-final weekend. We preview Manchester City v Southampton and Chelsea v Leeds, and look ahead to a big weekend at the bottom of the Premier League table.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The long-running game show sees more rival antique collectors test their skills at spotting a bargain. Two teams - the reds and the blues - set out with just £300 and one hour to scour an antiques fair and buy three items to sell at auction. An antiques expert guides each team, giving them hints and tips on what to buy in the hope they make more profit than their rivals when their items go under the hammer. Natasha Raskin Sharp presents this episode from Shepton Mallet in Somerset.

programme.title: London Marathon: My Reason to Run
programme.desc: Documentary feature about what motivates people to race the London Marathon. Gabby Logan looks ahead to the 45th London Marathon, one of the most eagerly anticipated events on the sporting calendar, and meets some of the athletes who make it so special. This year’s race is set to be the biggest ever staged. Gabby meets some of the 57,000 participants as they collect their individual race numbers. From the elite athletes to the charity champions and celebrities, we hear some uplifting, inspiring and entertaining stories from the amazing participants who each have their own special reason to run.

Bradley Walsh hosts as Fred Sirieix, Laura Whitmore, Eddie Kadi, Josh Widdicombe, Vanessa Feltz and Scarlett Moffatt try to fill in the missing blanks and win prizes for some lucky contestants.

programme.title: The Weakest Link

The celebrity contestants in this episode are Aisling Bea, La Voix, Ben Bailey Smith, Anna Richardson, Samantha Kinghorn, Will Best, Jason Byrne and Jacqui Joseph.

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Drama series about the staff and patients at Holby City Hospital’s emergency department, charting the ups and downs in their personal and professional lives. Our heroes step up as a mystery virus sweeps across Holby.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Who says sharing a flat is easy? Especially when your landlady is the woman you fancy, your life is a shambles, your cleaner is a bumbling idiot and your flatmate is a puddle of wetness. No wonder these guys are Not Going Out… Lee's favourite band is Oasis, which is why he rushed online to buy tickets to see them in concert. Now a year on he still can't come to terms with the fact that he lost his place in the queue and missed out.

A simple investigation will end up a bloody and deadly nightmare.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the lightning-fast, quick-fire quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. The contestants’ motivation is simple: stay out of the light, and they’ll be alright! Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz with six players competing to win £3,000.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship: Day 7, Afternoon Session
programme.desc: Coverage of the World Snooker Championship in Sheffield. Coverage of day 7 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Each week on Richard Osman's House of Games, four celebrities go head-to-head in a series of fast-paced trivia rounds. From quickfire quizzes to offbeat challenges, they test their general knowledge and mental agility in a fun, competitive setting. Only one can be crowned the week's champion and take home the coveted golden prize. The celebrities face their final quizzes, and the week's winner is crowned.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Japan for a breathtaking railway journey through the country that launched the magnificent Shinkansen bullet train. Michael leaves Kyushu by foot tunnel under the Kanmon straits to arrive on the main island of Honshu at the harbour town of Shimonoseki. Here, he encounters the deadly delicacy of pufferfish. Containing a neurotoxin with no known antidote, only highly trained chefs can prepare the fish. Will Michael give it a try? Travelling eastwards along the Honshu coast, Michael visits Hitachi’s Kasado Works, where the iconic Shinkansen high-speed trains are manufactured. He is thrilled to get inside the factory and see these amazing machines up close. To end this leg, Michael arrives by Shinkansen into Hiroshima, the city devastated by the atomic bomb dropped by US forces on 6th August 1945. Michael crosses the city to visit the Peace Memorial Park, where he meets Keiko Ogura, a bomb survivor who was just eight years old on that fateful day.

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Garden experts Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein are joined by guest judge and passionate gardener Dame Prue Leith, as they visit three stunning gardens. Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein lead the search to find Northern Ireland's greatest garden, with guest judge and passionate gardener Penny Lancaster.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Celebrating the great Scottish garden. Tips and advice to get the most out of your garden, with inspirational ideas from Scotland's most beautiful green spaces. There is a lot of productive gardening going on at Beechgrove as George Anderson and Ruth Vichos both work on projects that will bear fruit later in the year.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: A gardening show packed with ideas and timely reminders to get the most out of your garden. Monty is adding a dash of Mediterranean flair and flavour by planting lemons in pots. Sue Kent is in her Swansea garden, and Jamie Butterworth learns why the euphorbia is a bright star of the border.

programme.title: The Great British Menu
programme.desc: The country's top chefs compete to cook a fabulous banquet to celebrate the very best produce across the land. Each week, three chefs will battle it out in the kitchen for a chance to cook at the banquet, and in a new twist, a veteran of the competition will also taste and score their dishes daily. Only Two chefs will make it through to the end of the week. It’s the final of Great British Menu. The chefs cook a banquet for the great and the good of the British film industry in Liverpool. Who will be voted 2026's champion of champions?

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Performers both new and old take to the stage of London's Hammersmith Apollo theatre to present their stand-up routines and entertain the audience after an introduction from the host. Kerry Godliman introduces Suzi Ruffell and Loyiso Gola to the stage.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Newsnight is a weekly news and current affairs show with interviews with influential people and documentaries from around the world Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Matt Chorley.

programme.title: Don't look now
programme.desc: After the shockingly unexpected death of their daughter, John and Laura Baxter go to Venice to recuperate, but find themselves confronted by strange premonitory visions.

programme.desc: Coverage of the World Snooker Championship in Sheffield. Highlights from the seventh day of the World Snooker Championship in Sheffield.

programme.title: Panorama BBC
programme.desc: Investigative documentary series revealing the truth about the stories that matter. Judith Moritz examines how abuse, intimidation and violence aimed at Jewish communities has become more widespread, leaving many British Jews feeling fearful and under threat.

programme.title: The Great British Menu
programme.desc: The country's top chefs compete to cook a fabulous banquet to celebrate the very best produce across the land. programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: A weekly programme from the BBC World Service, bringing forensic journalism from all corners of the world. BBC Eye follows Kenyans at the centre of a legal battle to track down their British soldier fathers. And Selin Girit reports on Turkey's reaction to a widening regional conflict.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Piripenguins is a bright and upbeat new children’s series that follows five fun-loving penguins – Pancake, Nugget, Brinicle, Looph and Flutter – living life to the fullest on their very own remote iceberg. Comedic adventures see the colony facing everyday challenges in their frosty world and learning invaluable lessons about friendship, teamwork, and self-discovery. When Millie hosts a series of tough penguin games, Pancake and Nugget must stick together.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: The cosmic adventures of astronauts Cosima and Dad on Proxima B, where they meet larger-than-life Big Lizard! Cosima and Big Lizard race against time to watch a cosmic triple eclipse!

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: In a barn in rural Wisconsin, Weasy, an immature and carefree weasel, suddenly becomes the adoptive parent of two ducklings he accidentally hatched. Despite his teenage-like antics, he does his best to raise his energetic and cheerful little charges, who in turn embrace life in their unconventional family. The Chipmunk accidentally gets hit by a ball during a game of baseball. To make him feel better, the Ducklings feed him popcorn. The Chipmunk pretends to be sick to get more and more privileges.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: A comedy-quest adventure about five brave heroes brought together by a love of quests, picnics and frolics! The heroes learn not to frolic before the end of a quest.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: In a vast natural reserve in Canada, there is a small haven of human civilization: the forest ranger's house. As soon as the forest ranger is away, GRIZZY feels it's only natural that the house becomes his territory. And no other animal would dare to contest this privilege. Nobody? Except the Lemmings! They may look inoffensive, but there are a lot of them, and they want their part of the fun! The Lemmings use all the cans and tins they can get their hands on to build a beach slide which annoys Grizzy.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck is just trying to make rent and is always accepting new jobs, but his dreams of keeping a job for longer than a day inevitably crumble before him thanks to overbearing Frog When Duck and Frog have to change a lighthouse’s light bulb, they quickly become marooned on the island. With no food, and surrounded by water, they must try to find a way to survive.

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons: The Series
programme.desc: Two young adventurers on a thrilling quest visit new lands, unravel ancient mysteries and encounter Pokémon,and Poké Balls, they've never seen before Life is back to normal for Roy and friends, until wild Pokémon begin to act strangely...

programme.title: Bros in Control
programme.desc: The ultimate head-to-head contest, where best mates Adam Beales and Joe Tasker battle to be the best in the funniest, gungiest, and grossest games. Adam and Joe become real-life pop stars, with dance moves provided by The Next Step cast - then become their dogs!

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Nitro plays hilarious pranks on his fellow Gladiators. Legend, Cyclone, Nitro and Sabre return to compete at a hotel check-in. With a hidden earpiece, will they obey all silly instructions for the chance to win or tap out and risk losing?

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Packed full of amazing celeb guests, epic challenges and lols galore! Joel finds out how potatoes are turned into crisps and invents his own new flavour!

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Wildlife presenter Steve Backshall tracks down 60 of the world's deadliest animals. Steve Backshall goes in search of bat-hunting boas, meets a team working to save the deadly but cute slow loris, and he comes face to face with the king of snakes: the king cobra.

programme.desc: A panel of experts run through the afternoon's football results from the Premier League and beyond. Jason Mohammad and guests bring you all the goals, latest news, analysis, reaction and interviews from the afternoon’s biggest football matches.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and his team of experts invite members of the public to bring their antiques to be viewed and valued, with an option to sell at auction. This episode of the antiques series comes from Longleat in Wiltshire, home to a safari park and Longleat House. Paul Martin is joined by Michael Baggott and Claire Rawle.

programme.title: Murder on the Orient Express
programme.desc: An American businessman travelling on the Orient Express from Istanbul to Paris tries to hire Hercule Poirot to be his bodyguard as he has had death threats made against him. The sleuth refuses, but wakes up the following morning to learn the man has been stabbed to death while the train was held up by heavy snow - and there is no shortage of suspects among the passengers.

programme.title: Inside Britain's National Parks
programme.desc: A look at four of Britain’s 15 national parks, capturing these unique places and meeting the people who live and work in the New Forest, Pembrokeshire Coast, Dartmoor and Northumberland. The Northumberland National Park is characterised by a unique landscape of wild moors, gentle hills and deep valleys criss-crossed by clear rivers.

programme.title: Black British Music at the BBC
programme.desc: From Sade to Stormzy, Soul II Soul to So Solid Crew - an epic, electric and eclectic playlist covering pop, soul, drum and bass, ska and reggae. DJ Spoony selects a playlist of 40 great tracks that bring together some of the most inspiring names from the worlds of pop, soul, ska, reggae, rap and garage

programme.title: Sight & Sound in Concert
programme.desc: Vintage rock and pop performances from the archives Alan Black introduces Joan Armatrading in concert at the Hammersmith Odeon in 1977.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship: Day 8
programme.desc: Coverage of the World Snooker Championship in Sheffield. Highlights from day 8 of the World Snooker Championship in Sheffield.

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: Shannon, the naively romantic daughter of a crime family, meets Arran. A darkly comic story of passion, heartbreak and love laced with fear. Shannon finally meets a boy she likes. But dating is always tough when you’re the only daughter of a crime family. When her parents realise who Arran is, will she fight for him?

programme.title: McCartney: The Hunt for the Lost Bass
programme.desc: The disappearance of McCartney’s original Höfner bass over 50 years ago is one of rock ‘n’ roll’s great mysteries. This is the story of the iconic instrument and the quest to find it.

programme.title: Andi Oliver’s Fabulous Feasts
programme.desc: Andi goes to Cornwall to help a local chef put on a feast to thank NHS staff. Andi is in Bristol to help Jasper Thompson, a local man whose pioneering homeless project transforms shipping containers into temporary accommodation. He wants to raise awareness about his new community cafe, so who better than Andi to help him put on an unforgettable launch party with a Caribbean flavour? To entice locals to the event and kick off the cafe with a bang, Andi must put together a unique and delicious feast that draws on her and Jasper’s own roots in Antigua and Jamaica while also paying tribute to Bristol’s wider Caribbean heritage, using only the best local produce. The cafe itself needs a complete refurb, but can Jasper get it ready in time, and can Andi harness the Bristolians' unique creative spirit to make sure the event’s a roaring success?

Bloomberg Open Interest with Matt Miller and Dani Burger - delivers the market-moving news you need to know to get a jump start on the US trading day.

programme.title: Bloomberg Technology
programme.desc: The only daily live technology program with a focus on breaking news, market-moving reporting and analysis of global technology companies and stories.

programme.title: Bloomberg Real Yield
programme.desc: Focusing on global issues and events that directly affect debt markets. Host Jonathan Ferro discusses stories from the fixed income world with the biggest names in the field. A weekly show on fixed income with the biggest names in the field, covering sovereign and credit markets as investors grapple with fiscal, monetary and financial factors.

programme.title: Bloomberg: Balance of Power
programme.desc: A discussion on United States politics and policies as defined by President Joe Biden, Vice President Kamala Harris, and their administration provides insight into how these policies may shape the country through 2025. Bloomberg Balance of Power focuses on politics and policies on Capitol Hill, in President Trump's administration, the federal agencies and the courts.

programme.title: Bloomberg Businessweek Daily
programme.desc: Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec bring you reporting from the magazine that helps global leaders stay ahead, plus insight on the people, companies and trends shaping today's complex economy.

programme.title: Bloomberg Markets: The Close
programme.desc: This fast-paced programme is the quintessential market close show leading up to the final minutes and seconds before the closing bell on Wall Street with the latest news, data and expert analysis. Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld break down the day's top market-moving stories and trading action around the closing bell.

programme.title: Bloomberg: Balance of Power
programme.desc: A discussion on United States politics and policies as defined by President Joe Biden, Vice President Kamala Harris, and their administration provides insight into how these policies may shape the country through 2025. Bloomberg Balance of Power focuses on politics and policies on Capitol Hill, in President Trump's administration, the federal agencies and the courts.

programme.title: Bloomberg Wall Street Week
programme.desc: Bloomberg anchor David Westin is joined by economists, policy makers, chief executive officers, and influential and thought-provoking leaders for a round table discussion over the stock markets and various geopolitical topics.
